document.document_summary: This document is Issue 1733 of the Al-Kuwait Al-Youm Official Gazette, published on April 6, 2025, covering a wide range of official announcements and legal instruments from the State of Kuwait. It includes new decree laws, amendments to existing decrees concerning judicial promotions and property acquisition, and various decisions from the Capital Markets Authority regarding investment schemes, licensing fees, and regulatory compliance. The gazette also features numerous company announcements, such as liquidations, dissolutions, capital increases, and general assembly meeting minutes. Furthermore, it contains extensive sections on public tenders and auctions from various ministries and state-owned enterprises, covering medical supplies, infrastructure projects, and real estate. Finally, the issue includes official legal judgments, industrial registrations, and notices from the General Authority for Minors' Affairs.

chunks.title: Extending the Period Stipulated in Law No. 3 of 2016 Authorizing the Government to Withdraw an Amount from the General Reserve Fund
chunks.text_en: - Having reviewed the Constitution, - And Amiri Order issued on 2 Dhu al-Qi'dah 1445 AH, corresponding to May 10, 2024 AD, - And Law No. 24 of 1963 regarding the establishment of the Supreme Defense Council , amended by Decree-Law No. 53 of 1987, - And Decree-Law No. 31 of 1978 regarding the rules for preparing general budgets, monitoring their implementation, and the final account, and its amending laws, - And Decree-Law No. 27 of 1981 regarding increasing the amounts allocated for strengthening the country's defense, amended by Decree-Law No. 33 of 1987, - And Decree-Law No. 35 of 1988 regarding increasing the amounts allocated for strengthening the country's defense, - And Decree-Law No. 46 of 1992 regarding increasing the amounts allocated for strengthening the country's defense, and its amending laws, - And Law No. 3 of 2016 authorizing the Government to withdraw an amount from the General Reserve Fund , - And Decree No. 84 of 2024 concerning Ministerial Succession and Delegation , and its amending decrees, - And based on the presentation by the Minister of Defense , - And after the approval of the Council of Ministers / Majlis Al-Wuzara , - We have issued the following Decree-Law:

chunks.title: Article One
chunks.text_en: The period stipulated in Articles One and Two of the aforementioned Law No. 3 of 2016 shall be extended for an additional two fiscal years.

chunks.title: Article Two
chunks.text_en: The Prime Minister and the Ministers – each within their respective jurisdiction – shall implement this Decree-Law, and it shall come into effect from the date of its publication in the Official Gazette . Amir of Kuwait Mishal Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah Acting Prime Minister Fahad Yousef Saud Al-Sabah Minister of Defense Abdullah Ali Abdullah Al-Salem Al-Sabah

chunks.title: Regarding Decree-Law No. 61 of 2025 <orig>المرسوم بقانون رقم 61 لسنة 2025</orig> Extending the Period Stipulated in Law No. 3 of 2016 <orig>القانون رقم 3 لسنة 2016</orig> Authorizing the Government to Withdraw an Amount from the General Reserve Fund <orig>المال الاحتياطي العام</orig>
chunks.text_en: Law No. 3 of 2016 was issued, authorizing the Government to withdraw a specified sum from the General Reserve Fund to enhance the state's defense capabilities over a period of ten fiscal years, commencing from the fiscal year 2015/2016, in order to keep pace with regional developments and ensure the readiness of the armed forces . Given the approaching end of the period specified by the aforementioned Law, and the continued need to complete approved defense projects, it has become necessary to extend this period for an additional two fiscal years. This extension aims to enable the competent authorities to complete armament and military equipment programs in accordance with pre-determined plans, without exceeding the amount specified in the aforementioned Law No. 3 of 2016 , as no amendment has been made to the allocated expenditure volume; rather, the extension is limited to granting more time to complete existing defense projects. Provided that the Supreme Defense Council continues to exercise its powers as defined by the aforementioned Law No. 3 of 2016 , including supervising the allocation and disbursement of approved funds, and overseeing the implementation of contracts to ensure optimal use of financial resources and achievement of the prescribed defense objectives. And since the Amiri Order was issued on 2 Dhu al-Qa'dah 1445 AH, corresponding to 10/5/2024 AD, including a provision in Article (4) thereof that laws shall be issued by decree-laws, the accompanying draft Decree-Law has been prepared, consisting of two articles. Where Article One of the draft stipulated the extension of the period provided for in Articles One and Two of the aforementioned Law No. 3 of 2016 for an additional two fiscal years, and Article Two of the draft obligated both the Prime Minister and the Ministers to implement it and act upon it from the date of its publication in the Official Gazette . <footer> Issued at Seif Palace on: 27 Ramadan 1446 AH Corresponding to: 27 March 2025 AD </footer>

chunks.title: Amending Decree No. 139 of 2024 Regarding Judicial Promotions and Transfers

chunks.title: Article One
chunks.text_en: Renewal of the license for a collective investment scheme for Boubyan Multi-Asset Holding Fund , for a period of three years, renewable, effective from 2025/04/25.

chunks.title: Article Two
chunks.text_en: The competent authorities shall implement this decision, each within its jurisdiction, and it shall be effective from the date of its issuance and published in the Official Gazette. Head of Supervision Sector Ziad Yaqoub Yousef Al-Fulaij

chunks.title: Resolution No. (54) of 2025
chunks.text_en: Regarding the Exemption of Insurance Products from Collective Investment Schemes **Having reviewed:** - Law No. (7) of 2010 regarding the Establishment of the Capital Markets Authority and the Regulation of Securities Activities, and its Executive Regulations and amendments; - And the Board of Commissioners of the Capital Markets Authority Resolution No. (B.C.C.M.A.-9 of 2025) issued at its meeting No. (9) of 2025 held on 2025/03/26. **Decided the following:**

chunks.title: Article One:
chunks.text_en: Article (1-1-2) of Book Thirteen (Collective Investment Schemes) of the Executive Regulations of Law No. (7) of 2010 regarding the Establishment of the Capital Markets Authority and the Regulation of Securities Activities and their amendments, shall be amended as set forth in Attachment No. (1) to this Resolution.

chunks.title: Article Two:
chunks.text_en:
The competent authorities shall implement this Resolution, each within its jurisdiction, and it shall be effective from the date of its issuance and published in the Official Gazette. Acting Executive Director Othman Ibrahim Al-Essa Attachment No. 1: Amendment to Article 1-1-2 of Book Thirteen (Collective Investment Schemes) | No. | Book | Chapter | Article | Type of Amendment | Text Before Amendment | Text After Amendment | |:--|:-------|:-------|:-------|:---|:---|:---| | 1 | Thirteen | One | 1-1-2 | Amendment of Article | The following are not included in collective investment schemes: 1. Insurance contracts and related investment instruments, and investments managed as part of Takaful insurance contracts. 2. Investment accounts with Islamic banks subject to the supervision of the Central Bank. 3. Investment portfolios. 4. Contracts concluded between companies belonging to the same group. 5. Savings, pension, retirement, or benefit schemes established for the benefit of company employees. 6. Any other cases that the Authority decides to exclude from the scope of collective investment schemes. | The following are not included in collective investment schemes: 1. Insurance products whose purpose is to pay specific amounts due to death, disability, reaching a certain age, abandonment, or upon the expiry of the agreed term, or life insurance, without the investment results being connected to profit or loss. 2. Investment accounts subject to the supervision of the Central Bank. 3. Investment portfolios. 4. Contracts concluded between companies belonging to the same group. 5. Savings, pension, retirement, or benefit schemes established for the benefit of company employees. 6. Any other cases that the Authority decides to exclude from the scope of collective investment schemes. |
Issued on: April 2, 2025 AD Issued on: April 2, 2025 AD
AH – 2 – 7 of 2025) issued at its meeting No. (7) of 2025 held on 05/03/2025 AD. Decided the following:

chunks.title: Article One
chunks.text_en: Book Two (Capital Markets Authority ) of the Executive Regulations of Law No. (7) of 2010 regarding the establishment of the Capital Markets Authority and the regulation of securities activities and their amendments shall be amended in accordance with Annex No. (1) of this Decision.

chunks.title: Article Two
chunks.text_en:
The competent authorities shall implement this Decision, each within its jurisdiction, and it shall be effective from the date of its issuance and published in the Official Gazette. Acting Executive Director Othman Ibrahim Al-Essa Issued on: 02 April 2025 AD Annex No. (1) - Amendments to the Executive Regulations of the Capital Markets Authority Law | No. | Book | Article No. | Type of Amendment | Article After Amendment | Article Before Amendment | |:--|:-------|:---|:---|:---|:---| | 1. | Two | 1-2-6 | Article Amendment | A committee for receiving complaints and grievances shall be formed by a decision of the Board, consisting of no more than four members with expertise in financial, economic, and legal affairs, including a chairman and a vice-chairman of the committee. The committee shall receive complaints from any interested party regarding any error committed by any licensed person or entity subject to the Authority's supervision, and it shall also receive grievances regarding decisions issued by the Authority. The committee's deliberations shall be confidential, and its decisions shall be issued by majority vote; if votes are tied, the side with the chairman shall prevail. One of the Authority's employees specializing in any of the aforementioned specializations may also be selected as a member of this formation. | A committee for receiving complaints and grievances shall be formed by a decision of the Board, consisting of no more than four members with expertise in financial, economic, and legal affairs, including a chairman and a vice-chairman of the committee, for a period of two years renewable once. The committee shall receive complaints from any interested party regarding any error committed by any licensed person or entity subject to the Authority's supervision, and it shall also receive grievances regarding decisions issued by the Authority. The committee's deliberations shall be confidential, and its decisions shall be issued by majority vote; if votes are tied, the side with the chairman shall prevail. |
The committee's deliberations shall be confidential, and its decisions shall be issued by majority vote. If votes are tied, the side on which the chairperson is present shall prevail. Article Amendment Details | . | Second | 2-3-6 | Article Amendment | |:--|:-------|:------|:---| | | | | The Sharia Supervisory Advisory Board shall consist of at least five members who are experts specializing in Islamic financial transactions jurisprudence, law, economics, and administrative sciences, provided that the majority in the formation of this Board are experts specializing in Sharia. The Board shall issue a decision appointing them for a renewable period of two years. |
Decided the following:

chunks.title: Article One
chunks.text_en: Kuwait Financial Centre Company is licensed to privately market 35,000 shares (only thirty-five thousand shares) of MKZ Development 46 Company , established in the Cayman Islands, within the State of Kuwait , and the offer price shall be 1,000 Euros (only one thousand Euros) per share.

chunks.title: Article Two
chunks.text_en: The shares to be marketed within the State of Kuwait shall be offered to professional clients only, as per the text stipulated in Book One (Definitions) of the Executive Regulations of Law No. 7 of 2010 regarding the Establishment of the Capital Markets Authority and the Regulation of Securities Activities and their amendments. The entity receiving subscription requests shall be: - Kuwait Financial Centre Company .

chunks.title: Article Three
chunks.text_en: The objectives of the collective investment scheme shall be determined based on what is stated in the prospectus.

chunks.title: Article Four
chunks.text_en: The marketing license duration is one year, renewable annually, starting from the date of issuance of the Authority's license certificate and after payment of the prescribed fees.

chunks.title: Article Five
chunks.text_en: The prescribed fees shall be paid within one month from the date of issuance of this decision. In case of failure to pay the fees within the specified period, the decision shall be considered null and void.

chunks.title: Article Six
chunks.text_en: The competent authorities shall implement this decision, each within its jurisdiction. It shall be effective from the date of its issuance and published in the Official Gazette . <footer> Issued on: April 2, 2025 AD Head of Supervision Sector Ziad Yaqoub Yousef Al-Fulaij </footer>

chunks.title: Regarding Amending Certain Provisions of the Executive Regulations of Law No. (7) of 2010 Regarding the Establishment of the Capital Markets Authority <orig>هيئة أسواق المال</orig> and the Regulation of Securities Activities and their Amendments
chunks.text_en: Having reviewed: - Law No. (7) of 2010 regarding the establishment of the Capital Markets Authority and the regulation of securities activities, its executive regulations, and their amendments; - And on the two resolutions of the Board of Commissioners of the Capital Markets Authority Nos. (B.C.CMA 9-9 of 2025 and B.C.CMA 11-9 of 2025) in its meeting No. (9) of 2025 held on 26/03/2025; It has been decided as follows:

chunks.title: Article One:
chunks.text_en: Appendix No. (4) regarding the schedule of Authority service fees from Book Two (Capital Markets Authority ) of the Executive Regulations of Law No. (7) of 2010 regarding the establishment of the Capital Markets Authority and the regulation of securities activities and their amendments shall be amended in accordance with Annex No. (1) of this Resolution.

chunks.title: Article Two:
chunks.text_en: Book Three (Law Enforcement ) of the Executive Regulations of Law No. (7) of 2010 regarding the establishment of the Capital Markets Authority and the regulation of securities activities and their amendments shall be amended in accordance with Annex No. (2) of this Resolution.

chunks.title: Article Three:
chunks.text_en: Book Five (Securities Activities and Registered Persons ) of the Executive Regulations of Law No. (7) of 2010 regarding the establishment of the Capital Markets Authority and the regulation of securities activities and their amendments shall be amended in accordance with Annex No. (3) of this Resolution.

chunks.title: Article Four:
chunks.text_en: Both Appendix No. (7) "Application Form for Cancellation of Securities Activities ", and (11) "Application Form for Approval of Effective Control over a Licensed Person " from Book Five (Securities Activities and Registered Persons ) regarding the establishment of the Capital Markets Authority and the regulation of securities activities and their amendments shall be amended in accordance with Annex No. (4) of this Resolution.

chunks.title: Article Five:
chunks.text_en: The competent authorities shall implement this Resolution, each within its jurisdiction, and it shall be effective from the date of its issuance and published in the Official Gazette . <footer> Issued on: 3 April 2025 AD Acting Executive Director Othman Ibrahim Al-Essa </footer> Attachment (1) Schedule of Authority Services Fees Appendix No. (4) Schedule of Authority Services Fees

11: Withdrawal of Service Request (Refund of Paid Fee): 11-1 Without prejudice to the provisions for withdrawing service requests and refunding fees stipulated in the table above, the submitted service request may be withdrawn and the paid fee partially refunded in accordance with the provisions mentioned below, for some services marked with (**) in the table above. Withdrawal and fee refund do not apply to other services. 11-2 The service applicant or their legal representative may withdraw the service request, based on a request submitted to the Authority stating the reasons for withdrawal, and at the latest by the next business day following the day of submitting the service request. 11-3 The withdrawal request shall be decided upon within two business days of its submission, and the deadline for deciding on the request may be extended if the Authority deems it necessary, provided that the commencement of examining the service matter has not occurred. 11-4 The paid fee shall be partially refunded if the Authority approves the service withdrawal request, at a rate of 25% of the fee paid for some services listed in the fee schedule. The Authority shall decide on the resumption of the suspended or restricted activity of the licensed person within two months from the date of receiving the plan referred to in the previous item, provided that all required information and documents are complete. 3. The Authority shall notify the licensed person of its decision to approve or reject the plan. In the event that the Authority approves the plan, the Authority shall issue a decision to allow the licensed person to resume the activity at the time or lift the restriction, after payment of the prescribed fees.

chunks.title: Submission of Cancellation Application
chunks.text_en: The licensed person shall submit an application for license cancellation in accordance with the requirements of the Financial Securities Activities License Cancellation Form in Appendix No. (7) of this Book, attaching all required documents and information, provided that the documents include proof of notification to the Central Bank for entities subject to its supervision. The Authority may request – at any time after receiving the application – any additional documents or information if it deems it necessary. In the event that the licensed person fails to submit the required documents and data within the period specified by the Authority without an acceptable excuse from the Authority , the application shall be considered null and void.

chunks.title: Submission of an Application to Obtain the Authority's Approval for Effective Control over the Licensed Person According to the Following Procedures:
chunks.text_en:
1. The application shall be submitted according to the form in Appendix No. (11) of this Book, along with the payment of the prescribed fees, at least sixty (60) days before entering into arrangements or agreements that lead to effective control over the licensed person. The period commences from the date of completion of all required data and information. 2. The Authority shall decide on the application within a maximum period of sixty (60) days from the date of its submission, provided it is complete with any information or documents requested by the Authority . The Authority shall grant its approval to the application after ensuring that any effective control over the licensed person or ownership of shares or capital stakes will not hinder the supervision and oversight of the licensed person or lead to non-compliance with the Law and this Regulation. This is after the applicant pays the prescribed fees. In case the Authority rejects the application, the rejection decision must be reasoned.
Appendix No. (4) Appendix No. (7) Application Form for Cancellation of Financial Securities Activities License Contents of Appendix No. 7: Application for Cancellation of Financial Activities License | Section | Contents | |:--------|:---| | Section 1 | Instructions | | Section 2 | Applicant's Data | | Section 3 | License to be Cancelled | | Section 4 | Required Attachments and Documents |

chunks.title: 1. Instructions
chunks.text_en: * The licensed person shall fulfill all requirements stated in Appendix (27) - Instructions for Requesting Cancellation of License for Some or All Securities Activities - before submitting a request for cancellation of the securities activities license. * Submitting a request for license cancellation requires prior approval from the Extraordinary General Assembly on the proposal to cancel the license for some or all securities activities, provided that such cancellation is justified. * The licensed person is obligated to submit the items stated in Book Five concerning license cancellation upon the request of the licensed person. * The proposed cancellation date shall be at least six months after the date of submitting the application. * The Authority may request any additional information or documents or any clarifications from the licensed person during the review of their application. If the licensed person fails to provide the required documents and information within the period specified by the Authority in the notification without an acceptable excuse from the Authority , the application shall be considered null and void. * The Authority shall decide on the application within two months of fulfilling all required documents and information. * Provide the Authority with supporting documents for the application in case any requirement in this form or the required attachments does not apply to the applicant. * The applicant is obligated to comply with all procedures issued by the Authority required after the decision on the submitted application. * Units subject to the supervision of the Central Bank must adhere to the instructions regarding license cancellation as stipulated in the Memorandum of Understanding concluded between the Capital Markets Authority and the Central Bank . * The licensed person is obligated to notify the Authority in writing immediately upon the occurrence of any impediments in the procedures for canceling the securities activities license. * The licensed person is obligated to submit a license renewal application if they fail to comply with the specified timeframe for license cancellation. * The Authority may oblige the applicant for license cancellation to take all measures and precautions that ensure no harm is caused to their interests or the integrity of the financial system as a result of the license cancellation. * The licensed person may resort to the arbitration system at the Authority to settle disputes related to securities activities. * The licensed person must submit the cancellation request through the Authority's electronic portal and pay the prescribed fees. In case of any inquiries regarding the application submission or related matters, contact the Licensing and Registration Department at the Capital Markets Authority at this address: Kuwait - Sharq - Al Hamra Tower /20th Floor - Capital Markets Authority P.O. Box 3913 Al Safat 13040 Kuwait lrd@cma.gov.kw

chunks.title: Regarding Tender No. 2097004-RFP
chunks.text_en:
Expansion of (EWDP I & II) Stations for the Injection Maintenance Project EWDP I & II EXPANSION FOR INJECTION MAINTENANCE PROJECT - Kuwait Oil Company The Central Agency for Public Tenders announces the postponement of the deadline for submitting bids for the aforementioned tender to Sunday, 2025-05-06, instead of Sunday, 2025-04-06. Secretary General of the Central Agency for Public Tenders 52-2024/2025 | No. | Subject of Practice/Tender | Requesting Entity | Cost of Terms and Specifications Booklet Fees | Date and Place of Bid Opening | |:--|:---|:---|:---|:---| | 1 | Purchase of Licenses for Human Resources Automation System and Mobile Biometric Program | Information Systems Center | 75 K.D. | Day: Sunday, Corresponding to: 2025/4/20, Tenders Department, Financial Affairs Department, 6th Floor, 2:00 PM |
* The practice/tender document can be obtained starting from Sunday, 2025/3/30, and on subsequent days during official working hours of the Financial Affairs Department (Ministry Building located in Al-Riqqa area - 6th Floor - Tenders Department). The last deadline for submitting bids is Sunday, 2025/4/20, at noon. Bids received after this deadline will not be considered, noting that the mentioned fees are non-refundable. * All documents must be submitted on a non-copyable CD, except for the bid form and initial security, which must be submitted as hard copies. * Bids must be placed in the tenders box at the Tenders Department, along with an initial security deposit of (750 K.D. ) in the form of a letter of guarantee or a certified check approved by one of the accredited local banks in the State of Kuwait , unconditional and free of any reservations, and it must be valid throughout the bid validity period, which is (90 days). * All companies participating in the practice/tender are invited to attend the opening of envelopes at the date and place specified in the table above. Note: The practice/tender document can be withdrawn by specialized companies in this field, provided they are registered with the Ministry's Tenders Department, from 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM, with the submission of the 2025 registration card. Ministry's Electronic Tenders Website: (https://tenders.awqaf.gov.kw/Tender/Tender_Public_Practice)

chunks.text_en:
The Extraordinary General Assembly of National Financial Brokerage Company held its meeting on Thursday, March 20, 2025, at 12:00 PM at the company's headquarters located in NBK Tower – 34th Floor – Jaber Al-Mubarak Street and Al-Shuhada Street – Sharq Area , after the legal quorum for shareholder attendance was met with 50,000,000 shares, representing 100% in accordance with the law. In attendance: Mr. Tariq Abdul Latif Al-Shehab , Chairman of the Board of Directors Mr. Tariq Abdul Latif Al-Shehab – Chairman of the Board of Directors welcomed the attendees, and then the Extraordinary General Assembly proceeded to discuss the topics on the agenda and decided the following: **Item One:** The draft merger agreement by absorption between (K.S.C.) Public Minutes of the Extraordinary General Assembly Meeting Dated 2025/3/25 At exactly 12:45 PM on Tuesday, 2025/3/25, the Extraordinary General Assembly Meeting was held at the Company's head office. After the legal quorum was met with the attendance of shareholders owning and representing [191,944,794] shares out of the Company's total shares of [220,500,000] shares, i.e., 87.049%, and after reviewing the agenda, the following was approved: 1- Approval of increasing the Company's issued and paid-up capital from K.D. 22,050,000 to K.D. 23,600,000 , by issuing 15,500,000 shares equivalent to K.D. 1,550,000 at a value of (100) Fils per share, representing 7.029478%. These shares shall be distributed as free bonus shares to shareholders registered in the Company's books as of the end of the record date according to the timeline in the share entitlements disclosure form, and authorizing the Board of Directors to dispose of fractional shares arising from the distribution of bonus shares as it deems appropriate. 2- Approval of amending Article (6) of the Articles of Incorporation and Article (7) of the Articles of Association as follows: Text before amendment: The Company's authorized capital is set at K.D. 30,000,000 (Thirty Million Kuwaiti Dinars ) distributed over 300,000,000 shares, with each share valued at (100) Fils , and all shares are cash shares. The Company's issued and paid-up capital is set at K.D. 22,050,000 (Twenty-Two Million and Fifty Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ) distributed over 220,500,000 shares, with each share valued at (100) Fils , and all shares are cash shares. Text after amendment: The Company's authorized capital is set at K.D. 30,000,000 (Thirty Million Kuwaiti Dinars ) distributed over 300,000,000 shares, with each share valued at (100) Fils , and all shares are cash shares. The Company's issued and paid-up capital is set at K.D. 23,600,000 (Twenty-Three Million and Six Hundred Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ) distributed over 236,000,000 shares, with each share valued at (100) Fils , and all shares are cash shares. And after the approval of the competent regulatory authorities. The meeting concluded at 1:00 PM. Chairman of the Board of Directors Then proceeded to discuss the following agenda item: * Addition of a new clause to Article No. 47 of the Company's Articles of Association , to read as follows: * Text of Article 47 before amendment: * First: (10%) ten percent shall be deducted and allocated to the voluntary reserve account. The General Assembly may stop the deduction if the reserve exceeds half of the Company's capital. * Second: (1%) one percent shall be deducted and allocated to the Kuwait Foundation for the Advancement of Sciences account. * Third: A percentage shall be deducted and allocated to the voluntary reserve account, as proposed by the Board of Directors and approved by the General Assembly . This deduction shall be stopped by a resolution of the Ordinary General Assembly based on a proposal from the Board of Directors . * Fourth: A portion of the profits shall be deducted based on a proposal from the Board of Directors and its report to the General Assembly to meet the Company's obligations under labor laws . These funds may not be distributed to shareholders. * Fifth: The amount necessary for distributing an initial dividend of (5%) five percent to shareholders shall be deducted, as determined by the Board of Directors and approved by the General Assembly . * Text of Article 47 after amendment: * First: (10%) ten percent shall be deducted and allocated to the voluntary reserve account. The General Assembly may stop the deduction if the reserve exceeds half of the Company's capital. * Second: (1%) one percent shall be deducted and allocated to the Kuwait Foundation for the Advancement of Sciences account. * Third: A percentage shall be deducted and allocated to the voluntary reserve account, as proposed by the Board of Directors and approved by the General Assembly . This deduction shall be stopped by a resolution of the Ordinary General Assembly based on a proposal from the Board of Directors . * Fourth: A portion of the profits shall be deducted based on a proposal from the Board of Directors and its report to the General Assembly to meet the Company's obligations under labor laws . These funds may not be distributed to shareholders. * Fifth: The amount necessary for distributing an initial dividend of (5%) five percent to shareholders shall be deducted, as determined by the Board of Directors and approved by the General Assembly . * Sixth: After the foregoing, an amount determined by the Ordinary General Assembly , not exceeding (10%) ten percent of the remainder, shall be allocated for the Board of Directors' remuneration . * Seventh: The remaining profits shall then be distributed to shareholders as an additional dividend , or carried forward to the next year based on a proposal from the Board of Directors , or allocated to establish a general reserve fund or an extraordinary depreciation fund . * Eighth: The Ordinary General Assembly may, based on a proposal from the Board of Directors , distribute dividends to shareholders at the end of the financial year , or semi-annually. Furthermore, by a resolution of the Ordinary General Assembly determined annually, the Board of Directors may be authorized in advance to distribute dividends semi-annually and to determine the timeline for this distribution. The validity of this distribution is conditional upon it being from actual profits, in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles , and that this distribution does not affect the Company's paid-up capital. The meeting concluded at 11:15 AM. Vice Chairman of the Board of Directors and Chief Executive Officer Heep International Real Estate Company (K.S.C.) Closed Minutes of the Extraordinary General Assembly Meeting of the Company's Shareholders On Wednesday, March 19, 2025, at 1:00 PM, the Extraordinary General Assembly Meeting of Heep International Real Estate Company was held at the Company's premises located in Kuwait – Al-Qibla Area – Plot 11 – Parcel 9 – Building 2 – Mezzanine Floor, Office No. 1. The meeting was chaired by the Chairman of the Board of Directors , Mr. Zaid . They approved the following: Ratification of the previous agenda minutes, which was approved and ratified. Item One: Amendment of Article No. 29 in the Articles of Association and Bylaws. Text of Article 29 of the Bylaws before amendment: - The Board of Directors shall elect by secret ballot a Chairman of the Board and a Vice Chairman . The Chairman of the Board of Directors shall represent the company in its relations with third parties and before the judiciary, in addition to the other powers specified in the contract. The signature of the Board of Directors shall be considered the company's signature in its relations with third parties, and he shall implement the Board's decisions and adhere to its recommendations. The Vice Chairman shall act in his absence or if there is an impediment preventing him from exercising his powers. Text of Article 29 after amendment: - The Board of Directors shall elect by secret ballot a Chairman of the Board and a Vice Chairman . The Chairman of the Board of Directors shall have the broadest powers to manage the company and carry out all actions required for the company's management in accordance with its purposes. This authority shall not be limited except by what is stipulated by law, these Bylaws, or the resolutions of the General Assembly . The Board of Directors may borrow, sell, and mortgage the company's real estate, or provide guarantees, arbitration, conciliation, donations, and open and close bank accounts. The discussion took place, and all attendees approved the aforementioned amendment to the article's text. In conclusion, the Chairman of the Board of Directors thanked the attendees and concluded the Extraordinary General Assembly meeting at 2:00 PM. Chairman of the Board of Directors Kuwait International Takaful Insurance Company Minutes of the Extraordinary General Assembly Meeting Held on 2025/3/23 The Extraordinary General Assembly of Kuwait International Takaful Insurance Company met at the company's headquarters located on Arabian Gulf Street , Ahmed Tower , at 11:00 AM on Sunday, 2025/03/23, with 100% attendance. The meeting of the General Assembly was chaired by the Chairman of the Board of Directors of the company, with the presence of: 1 - Mr. Ghanem Miz'al Fahad Al-Shammari , Chairman of the Board of Directors 2 - Mr. Abdulaziz Saud Al-Mutairi , Vice Chairman of the Board of Directors 3 - Ms. Sahar Majed Faour , Secretary 4 - Mr. Fahad Abdulrahman Al-Badiwi , Gulf Custody Company The General Assembly ratified the following items: 1 - Amendment of Article No. (14) of the Bylaws: Text before amendment: The company shall be managed by a Board of Directors composed of (3) three members, from whom the entity or entities authorized to appoint representatives to the Board of Directors shall appoint members in proportion to their shareholding in the company, and the General Assembly shall elect the remaining members by secret ballot. Text after amendment: The company shall be managed by a Board of Directors composed of (5) five members, from whom the entity or entities authorized to appoint representatives to the Board of Directors shall appoint members in proportion to their shareholding in the company, and the General Assembly shall elect the remaining members by secret ballot. 2 - Amendment of the text of Article No. (5) of the Articles of Association and Article No. (4) of the Bylaws: Text before amendment: The purposes of the company are: The purposes of the company are as stated below, provided that all company transactions are subject to the provisions of the law in accordance with the provisions of Islamic Sharia , and what does not conflict with the regulating laws and decisions. 1 - Fire insurance. 2 - Accident insurance. 3 - Marine and aviation insurance. 4 - Engineering insurance (equipment, machinery, devices, and electronics). 5 - Motor vehicle insurance (compulsory / supplementary). 6 - Civil liability insurance towards third parties or third-party property. 7 - Health insurance (for two years) covering treatment expenses and surgical operations. The company shall engage in all cooperative Takaful insurance activities and what is analogous thereto and related to any activity required for its practice, and carry out insurance and reinsurance operations, conclude all contracts, and undertake all legal actions it deems necessary to achieve its purposes. To invest all or part of the company's movable or immovable funds in various fields it deems appropriate and in a manner that does not conflict with the regulating laws and decisions related to insurance. Providing consultations and conducting technical studies in the field of insurance and insurance management for companies or others in the field of Takaful insurance . Owning and acquiring the right to dispose of all movable and immovable funds or any part thereof, or any privileges that the company deems necessary and appropriate for the nature of its work and essential for the growth of its funds. The company shall directly conduct the aforementioned activities in the State of Kuwait and abroad, either as a principal or by agency. The company may have an interest or participate in any way with entities that engage in similar activities or that may assist it in achieving its purposes in Kuwait or abroad, and it may acquire or merge with such entities. Text after amendment: The purposes of the company are as stated below, provided that all company transactions are subject to the provisions of the law in accordance with the provisions of Islamic Sharia , and what does not conflict with the regulating laws and decisions, and what is required to maintain the objective of achieving cooperation and solidarity among participants. Approved Activities of the Company | No. | Activity Code | Activity Name | |:--|:---|:---| | 1 | 651107 | Non-Life Takaful Insurance and Fund Formation. | | 2 | 681091 | Ownership of Real Estate and Movables for the Company's Benefit. | | 3 | 649918 | Investment of Financial Surpluses by investing them in financial portfolios managed by specialized companies and entities. | | 4 | 649917 | Buying and Selling Shares and Bonds for the Company's Account. | | 5 | 660004 | Providing Administrative and Technical Services to Insurance Companies. |
3 - Amendment of Article No. (25) of the Bylaws: Text before amendment: - Without prejudice to the provisions of Companies Law No. 25 of 2012 and its executive regulations Text After Amendment: The liquidation of the company shall be carried out in accordance with the provisions of Companies Law No. 1 of 2016 , its amendments, and its executive regulations. - 8 Amendment of Article No. (53) of the Articles of Association: Text Before Amendment: The provisions of Commercial Companies Law No. 25 of 2012 , its amendments, and its executive regulations shall apply to all matters not specifically provided for in the Memorandum of Association or in these Articles. Text After Amendment: The provisions of Commercial Companies Law No. 1 of 2016 , its amendments, and its executive regulations shall apply to all matters not specifically provided for in the Memorandum of Association or in these Articles. After reviewing and discussing the items on the agenda, the aforementioned resolutions and amendments were adopted and unanimously approved. At the conclusion of the meeting, Mr. / Chairman of the Board of Directors and Chief Executive Officer thanked the attendees, and the Extraordinary General Assembly meeting concluded at 12:00 PM on the same day. Chairman of the Board of Directors Dunya Holding Company (K.S.C.C.) (Closed) Minutes of the Extraordinary General Assembly Meeting The Extraordinary General Assembly of the shareholders of Dunya Holding Company - a Kuwaiti Shareholding Company (Holding) - held its extraordinary meeting to add a new article to the company's Articles of Association, at 10:45 AM on Monday, March 24, 2025, at the company's headquarters located in Shuwaikh Industrial Area Three - Block A - Plot 98 - Mezzanine. The meeting was chaired by Mr. Ali Abdul Hamid Al-Qattan - Vice Chairman of the Board of Directors and Chief Executive Officer. Also present at the meeting were: - Mr. Fahad Abdul Rahman Al-Badiwi - representing Gulf Custody Company - in my capacity as Board Secretary. - Shareholders holding 21,000,000 shares, in person and by proxy, representing 100% of the total paid-up share capital of the company, amounting to 21,000,000 shares. After the announcement of the quorum for the Extraordinary General Assembly meeting, the Chairman of the session opened the meeting, welcoming the attendees, and then proceeded to present the items on the agenda of the Extraordinary General Assembly. Accordingly, after reviewing the agenda, the following resolution was adopted: * Approval was given to add a new article to the company's Articles of Association, to be numbered (76), with the following text: The shareholder's right to dispose of their shares shall be restricted by the following two limitations: 1- Requiring a right of first refusal for the company's shareholders to purchase shares that their owner wishes to sell. 2- Requiring the Board of Directors' approval for the share purchaser. Dispositions referred to in Companies Law No. 1 of 2016 are exempted from these two limitations. - Without prejudice to the provisions of Companies Law No. 1 of 2016 and its executive regulations, the Ordinary General Assembly shall determine the remuneration of the Board of Directors' members. - 4 Amendment of Article No. (29) of the Articles of Association, Clause No. 4: Text Before Amendment: - Any modern communication means specified in the executive regulations of Companies Law No. 25 of 2012 and its amendments. The Ministry of Commerce and Industry must be notified in writing of the agenda, date, and place of the meeting, generally at least, for its representative to attend. Text After Amendment: - Any modern communication means specified in the executive regulations of Companies Law No. 1 of 2016 and its amendments. The Ministry of Commerce and Industry must be notified in writing of the agenda, date, and place of the meeting, generally at least, for its representative to attend. - 5 Amendment of Article No. (36) of the Articles of Association: Text Before Amendment: The Annual General Assembly shall convene upon an invitation from the Board of Directors within three months following the end of the fiscal year, at the time and place designated by the Board of Directors. The Board may invite the General Assembly to meet whenever necessary. The Board of Directors must issue an invitation for the General Assembly to meet upon a reasoned request from a number of shareholders owning ten percent of the company's capital, or upon a request from the auditor, within fifteen days from the date of the request. The agenda shall be prepared by the inviting party. The provisions related to the founding assembly stipulated in Companies Law No. 25 of 2012 and its amendments shall apply to the procedures for inviting the General Assembly, the quorum for attendance, and voting. Text After Amendment: The Annual General Assembly shall convene upon an invitation from the Board of Directors within three months following the end of the fiscal year, at the time and place designated by the Board of Directors. The Board may invite the General Assembly to meet whenever necessary. The Board of Directors must issue an invitation for the General Assembly to meet upon a reasoned request from a number of shareholders owning ten percent of the company's capital, or upon a request from the auditor, within fifteen days from the date of the request. The agenda shall be prepared by the inviting party. The provisions related to the founding assembly stipulated in Companies Law No. 1 of 2016 and its amendments shall apply to the procedures for inviting the General Assembly, the quorum for attendance, and voting. - 6 Amendment of Article No. (51) of the Articles of Association: Text Before Amendment: The company shall be dissolved for one of the reasons stipulated in Companies Law No. 25 of 2012 , its amendments, and its executive regulations. Text After Amendment: The company shall be dissolved for one of the reasons stipulated in Companies Law No. 1 of 2016 , its amendments, and its executive regulations. - 7 Amendment of Article No. (52) of the Articles of Association: 8- If, upon studying the tender, the Ministry finds any error or omission, or if the technical documents or bills of quantities reveal anything that affects the categories of its bid or the value of the works, the tenderer must seek clarification from the Ministry of Education before submitting its bid. The Ministry will study these observations and inquiries, respond to them, and rectify any error, omission, ambiguity, or contradiction (if any). This shall not entail any additional responsibility or obligation on the Ministry. 9- Tender documents shall be deposited in the bids box at the Supplies and Warehouses Department in South Subhan area during official working hours, no later than (10) ten days from the date of announcement publication, at 12:00 PM sharp, according to the decision issued by the Ministry regarding changing or extending the closing date. No bid received after the stipulated closing date will be accepted, nor will any amendment to the prices of submitted bids received after bid submission be considered. 10- The tenderer must fill in the attached tables and submit a statement of all necessary supplies for executing the works subject of the tender – if any – within the same bid documents submitted. The Ministry reserves the right to reject the bid unless it includes the filled tables in accordance with public interest, with the necessity of adhering to what is stated in the technical specifications and conditions of the tender. 11- The tenderer must submit all bank guarantees related to this tender in Arabic. 12- A preliminary meeting will be held five days after the announcement. 3- The shareholder must notify the company of the terms of sale before disposing of their shares. The disposal of shares shall not be effective until ten days have elapsed from the date of notification without any shareholder submitting a request to purchase the shares. If a shareholder comes forward to purchase the shares, it must be at the price stated in the terms of sale. 4- Without prejudice to the provisions regarding the company's purchase of its own shares, the Board of Directors, in case of rejecting the buyer's person, must purchase the shares for the company's account within ten days from the date of notifying the Board of Directors of the approval request. In this case, the purchase shall be at the price agreed upon by the shareholder for selling their shares. (All of the above is subject to the approval of the competent regulatory authorities) Before the end of the meeting, the Chairman of the session, Mr. Ali Abdul Hamid Al-Qattan , thanked the attendees and concluded the ordinary general assembly meeting at 11:00 AM on the same day. Ministry of Education Re-issuance of Tender No. M.M / 2024/31-2025 The Ministry of Education announces the issuance of a (public) tender for leasing 20 half-lorries with drivers and handling workers, in accordance with the technical conditions and specifications in the tender documents. General Guidelines for the Tender: 1- The tender is (public) for approved tenderers with the Ministry of Education . 2- It is a condition for those applying for this tender that they must be specialized institutions/companies within Kuwait in the works subject of the tender, registered in the Commercial Register and the Kuwait Chamber of Commerce and Industry for the current year, and a certificate to that effect, dated concurrently with the tender issuance date, must be attached to their bid. 3- This tender is (non-divisible), and the bidder is not entitled to submit alternative offers. 4- The financial cost for obtaining a copy of the tender documents is (75) seventy-five Kuwaiti Dinars only. 5- The bid must be accompanied by an initial security deposit of no less than (K.D. 2000) two thousand Kuwaiti Dinars only. This security must be in the form of a certified check or a letter of guarantee from a bank accredited in the State of Kuwait , issued in the name of the bidder and in favor of the Ministry of Education , and not subject to any restriction or condition. Bids not accompanied by this security, or accompanied by security that violates any of the mentioned regulations, will not be considered. The security must be valid for the required bid validity period. 6- The total duration of the contract is three months from the date of signing the contract, during which the contractor is committed to executing the tender works in accordance with the general and special conditions, provisions, and technical specifications in the tender documents. Minutes of the Meeting of the Births and Deaths Committee at its session No. 9/2025, held on 16/3/2025 Formed by Ministerial Decision No. 1650/2021 The Births and Deaths Committee , stipulated in Article 15 of Law No. 36/93 and formed by Ministerial Decision No. 1650/2021, met. Mr. / Director of the Central Register Department for Births and Deaths | Chairman Mr. / Head of the General Medical Council | Member Mr. / Head of the Research and Opinion Department | Member Mr. / Head of the Registration Department | Member Mr. / Head of the Deaths Department | Member Mr. / Legal Researcher | Member Mr. / Head of the Records Department | Member Ms. Alia Al-Saffar acted as the Committee Secretary. The Committee reviewed applications from No. ((115)) to No. ((127)) in the attached list, pertaining to data amendments in birth and death registers for dropped birth and death registration applications. The Committee, after its review and ensuring the completeness of documents and records, decided to announce the amendments and applications in the Official Gazette (Al-Kuwait Al-Youm ) in two consecutive issues. This is so that if the legal period (fifteen days) from the date of the last announcement expires and no objection is submitted, the amendments will be made in the registers and birth and death certificates will be issued. chunks.title: Announcement of Property Sale by Public Auction
chunks.text_en: The Clerk's Office at the General Court announces the public auction of the property described below, on Monday, 5/5/2025 CE, in Hall 48, on the second floor of the Palace of Justice , at 9:00 AM, in execution of the court judgment issued in lawsuit No. 2024/57 Sales/1.

chunks.title: Filed by:
chunks.text_en: Jameelah Badi Rashid Al-Fadhli .

chunks.title: Against:
chunks.text_en: 1- Bushra Thaqeel Rashid Al-Ajmi . 2- Ahmed Abd Sultan Shallash . 3- Kuwait Credit Bank . 4- Director of the Real Estate Registration Department in his capacity. Property Description (According to the attached Certificate of Description) Property Document No. 5826/2016, located in Al-Na'eem area - Plot (227), Block No. (1), Plan M/37704 - with an area of 400m², by public auction with a base price of 420,000 K.D. (Four Hundred Twenty Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ). ** According to the Certificate of Description: Private residence consisting of a ground floor, first floor, and a partial second floor. There is an unlicensed elevator, and violation report No. 5431 was issued on 1/2/2024 CE. The violation is still ongoing, and this is for your information. ** According to the Certificate of Description: The disputed property located in Al-Na'eem area - Block 1 - Street 18 - Plot 227 - House 3 at the time of inspection is as follows: - The disputed property is a residential plot located on a single cul-de-sac street, bordered by neighbors on 3 sides, and consists of two and a half floors with a white mosaic facade. - The property's area is 400m² according to the document. - The disputed property's air conditioning is central, with some units being conventional. - Ordinary interior finishes. - Bathroom and washbasins - room with bathroom - preparatory kitchen - main kitchen. - Ground Floor: Reception hall with bathroom and washbasins - living room - diwaniya with bathroom and washbasins - driver's room - room with bathroom - preparatory kitchen - main kitchen. - First Floor: Master bedroom with bathroom and dressing room - 4 rooms with two bathrooms - preparatory kitchen - living room open to the corridor. - Half Floor with Roof: Room - bathroom - preparatory kitchen - hall. Second: Auction Conditions: First: The auction shall commence at the base price. Participation in the auction requires payment of at least one-fifth of that price by a certified check from the drawee bank or by a letter of guarantee from one of the banks in favor of the Execution Department at the Ministry of Justice . Second: The bidder whose offer is approved by the judge must deposit the full approved price, expenses, and registration fees at the time of the sale session. Third: If the bidder whose offer was approved does not deposit the full price, they must deposit at least one-fifth of the price; otherwise, the bidding shall be re-opened at their expense in the same session based on the price at which the sale was previously awarded. Fourth: If the bidder deposits in the subsequent session, the auction award judgment shall be against them, unless someone comes forward in that session who accepts to purchase with a ten percent increase, accompanied by the deposit of the full auction price. In this case, the bidding shall be re-opened in the same session based on this price. This is the announcement for bidding in the same session based on this price.

chunks.title: Firstly: Property Descriptions according to the attached Certificate of Descriptions <orig>شهادة الأوصاف</orig>.
chunks.text_en: - Property Document No. 2023/67 Fadel Samoon Restaurant . The plaintiff's agent stated that plot number 144 is completely identical to plot number 143 in its components. He also stated that the ground floor consists of a guard's room with a bathroom and kitchen, an electrical room, a room with its bathroom, and two identical apartments in terms of components, each consisting of two rooms, a hall, a bathroom, and a kitchen. He further stated that each floor has four apartments: two similar to each other, each consisting of two rooms, a hall, a bathroom, and a kitchen, and the other two similar, each consisting of one room, a hall, a bathroom, and a kitchen. He also stated that the basement is closed and contains nothing. The expert conducted an inspection of an apartment on the third floor and an apartment on the sixth floor, and it was found that they conform to the plaintiff's agent's statement regarding the apartments' components. The expert also found that the property's air conditioning is central, the walls have ordinary paint, and the roof has a designated area for the air conditioning unit. The plaintiff's agent stated that the monthly rent for each apartment ranges from 200 K.D. to 250 K.D. . Description of Property Ten (According to the Attached Description Certificate) Property of Document No. 2023/2285, located in Al-Jabriya area – Plot (49), Parcel No. (1), Plan M/30777, with an area of 1000m², to be sold by public auction at a base price of 1,650,000 K.D. (One Million Six Hundred Fifty Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ). The property is an investment residential building consisting of (ground floor + 5 repeated floors + roof) and there are violations, namely: 1- an additional apartment on the fifth floor, 2- closure of all balconies. ** According to the Expert's Report: The disputed property is an old building located on a corner at internal street No. (8) and street No. (7), with an area of 1000 m² as stated in document No. Description of Property Five (According to the Attached Description Certificate) Property of Document No. 2023/1931, located in Al-Funaitis area – Plot (140), Parcel No. (8), Plan M/37882, with an area of 400m², to be sold by public auction at a base price of 380,000 K.D. (Three Hundred Eighty Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ). It is located on a single street, No. 815, bordered by a neighbor on one side and vacant land on two sides. Description of Property Six (According to the Attached Description Certificate) Property of Document No. 2023/1919, located in Al-Funaitis area – Plot (141), Parcel No. (8), Plan M/37882, with an area of 400m², to be sold by public auction at a base price of 480,000 K.D. (Four Hundred Eighty Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ). It is located on a corner at street No. 815, bordered by vacant land. Description of Property Seven (According to the Attached Description Certificate) Property of Document No. 2023/2301, located in Al-Jabriya area – Plot (143), Parcel No. (A B), Plan M/30777, with an area of 1000m², to be sold by public auction at a base price of 1,650,000 K.D. (One Million Six Hundred Fifty Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ). The property is an investment residential building consisting of a basement + ground floor + 6 repeated floors + roof + shop, and there are violations, namely: corners on the ground floor and closure of balconies. ** According to the Expert's Report: The disputed property is an old building located on a corner at two streets, numbers (1 and 3), bearing number 14, with an area of 1000 m² as stated in document No. 2023/2303. It consists of a basement, a ground floor, and six additional floors, and includes a courtyard, an elevator, stairs, and parking spaces. Its exterior cladding is made of marble, and it has a structurally separate shop on the ground floor named Seven Fun Company for car electrical repair. The plaintiff's agent stated that the property is fully leased and he cannot enable the expert to inspect the apartments. He also stated that the components of the property subject of the lawsuit are as ..2285 and 2023, with its area, consisting of a closed ground floor, (5) repeated floors, and a roof. It has an elevator and parking spaces, and its exterior cladding is made of marble. It also has a structurally separate shop on the ground floor named "Ajrat Al-Rumh" . - The plaintiff stated that the property is fully leased and he cannot enable the expert to inspect the apartments. He also stated that the components of the property subject of the lawsuit are as follows: 1. The suspended ground floor contains (2) apartments, identical in terms of components, each consisting of a room, a hall, a bathroom, and a kitchen. 2. The first and second floors are identical, and each floor consists of (4) apartments, identical in terms of components, each consisting of a room, a hall, a bathroom, and a kitchen. 3. The third floor consists of (2) apartments, identical in terms of components, each consisting of a room, a hall, a bathroom, and a kitchen, and (2) apartments, identical in terms of components, each consisting of two rooms, a hall, a bathroom, and a kitchen. 4. The fourth and fifth floors are identical, and each floor consists of (4) apartments, identical in terms of components, each consisting of a room, a hall, a bathroom, and a kitchen. The plaintiff's agent stated that the air conditioning of the disputed property is central, and the monthly rent for each apartment ranges from 200 KD to 350 KD , and the rent for the shop ("Ajrat Al-Rumh" ) is 600 KD per month.

chunks.title: Description of the Eleventh Property (According to the Attached Description Certificate)
chunks.text_en: Property of Document No. 2023/2292 located in Al-Jabriya area – Plot (5) Piece No. (1) A, Plan M/30777, with an area of 750m², offered at public auction for a base price of 860,000 KD (Eight Hundred Sixty Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ). The property is located within a private residential area, consisting of a ground floor and a first floor.

chunks.title: According to the Expert's Report:
chunks.text_en:
The disputed property is a medium-built private residence located on two streets (front and back) and an alley (Street No. 14 and Main Street Abdullah Ali Dashti ), consisting of a ground floor, a first floor, and a roof. It has a courtyard surrounding the house, a garden, and a setback overlooking Abdullah Ali Dashti Street. Its area is 750m² as stated in Document No. 2023/2292. The property has (2) main entrances. The ground floor consists of (3) interconnected halls, an office room with its bathroom, a bathroom with washbasins, a diwaniya , a kitchen, and a maid's room with its bathroom. The first floor has a hall and Secondly: The person whose bid is approved by the judge must deposit the full approved price, expenses, and registration fees immediately upon the sale session being held. Thirdly: If the person whose bid is approved does not deposit the full price, they must deposit at least one-fifth of the price; otherwise, the bidding will be reopened at their expense in the same session based on the price at which the sale was previously awarded. Fourthly: If the bidder deposits the price in the subsequent session, the auction will be awarded to them, unless someone comes forward in that session willing to purchase with a ten percent increase, accompanied by the deposit of the full auction price. In this case, the bidding will be reopened in the same session based on this new price. Fifthly: If the initial bidder fails to deposit the full price in the subsequent session, and no one comes forward to bid with a ten percent increase, the bidding will immediately be reopened at their expense based on the price at which it was previously awarded to them in the preceding session. In this session, no bid not accompanied by a full deposit of its value will be considered. The defaulting bidder shall be liable for any shortfall in the property's price. Sixthly: In all cases, the successful bidder shall bear the transfer and property registration fees, execution procedure expenses amounting to 200 Kuwaiti Dinars , legal and expert fees, and advertising and publication costs for the sale in daily newspapers. Seventhly: This announcement shall be published in accordance with the law, and those directly responsible for the sale procedures shall do so at their own responsibility, without the Department of Clerks at the General Court bearing any liability. Eighthly: The successful bidder acknowledges that they have inspected the property in a manner that negates any ignorance of its condition. Notice: 1- This sale announcement shall be published in the Official Gazette in accordance with Article 266 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 2- The auction award judgment is subject to appeal within seven days from the date of its pronouncement, in accordance with Article 277 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 3- The last paragraph of Article 276 of the Code of Civil Procedure states that "If the person whose ownership has been divested is residing in the property, they shall remain therein as a tenant by operation of law, and the successful bidder shall be obligated to draw up a lease contract in their favor at a fair market rent." President of the General Court Announcement of Public Auction for Property Sale The Department of Clerks at the General Court announces the public auction sale of the property described below, on Monday, 5/5/2025 CE, in Hall 48, on the second floor of the Palace of Justice , at 9:00 AM, in execution of the court judgment issued in lawsuit No. 2023/183 Sales 1 and related lawsuits. For Inspection: On 12/4/2023 , we moved to Al-Jahra – Al-Naseem area – Block 1 – Street 28 – Plot No. 256 House 21, in the presence and guidance of the plaintiff personally, with the defendants or their representatives failing to appear, and in the presence of the expert, Professor/ Saad Al-Dukhani . The inspection was conducted as follows: - The disputed property is a plot located at the aforementioned address, consisting of a ground floor and a first floor, situated on a main street and a back street, and externally clad with beige stone. - The expert was unable to inspect the property from the inside after knocking on the door and the house not being opened. It was found by the expert that the main door was locked with an external lock. - Mr. Expert Professor/ Saad Al-Dukhani identified 2- The auction award ruling is appealable within seven days from the date of its pronouncement, in accordance with Article 277 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 3- The last paragraph of Article 276 of the Code of Civil Procedure states that "If the expropriated owner is residing in the property, they shall remain therein as a tenant by operation of law, and the successful bidder shall be obligated to issue a lease contract in their favor at the market rent ." Chief Justice of the General Court ** According to the description certificate : - The property is a private residence consisting of a ground floor + first floor, and there is a violation related to construction with light materials. ** According to the expert's report : On 6/6/2022, the expert team moved to Sabah Al-Salem area, Block 11, Plot 20. In the presence of Mr. Sa'ad Al-Daihani , the knowledge expert , and with the guidance and enablement of the third and fourth defendants, as the plaintiff failed to attend despite being aware of the session's date, as did the rest of the defendants. The expert team notes the discrepancy in number 8983, which states that the area of the disputed plot is 125,300 m², and also reviewed the sketch plan of the property by the plaintiff's agent in preparation for the necessary valuation of the property according to market price. The disputed property is located on a single internal street, surrounded by neighbors on both sides, and overlooks Main Street No. 207 from the back, named after Mohammed bin Hamada Al-Ajmi . The disputed property is an old government house, consisting of a ground floor, first floor, and a roof, covered with pink Sigma coating. It has two entrances, front and back, and the rear setback is utilized as a garden and parking. The third defendant enabled the expert team to enter and inspect, where the following was observed: - The ground floor consists of a small courtyard overlooking a diwaniya with a bathroom and washbasins, a driver's room with its entrance from Street No. 1, a kitchen + two bathrooms + two living rooms + a storage room under the stairs. - The first floor consists of two apartments: the first consists of a master bedroom + small living room + bedroom + bathroom - and the second apartment consists of 3 bedrooms + two bathrooms + kitchen. - The roof contains a master bedroom. - The property's finishes are as follows: ceramic floors, regular painted walls, spot lighting and suspended fixtures, and unit air conditioning. The expert team and the knowledge expert inspected the property from outside and inside. The knowledge expert also reviewed property document No. 8983, which states that the area of the disputed plot is 125,300 m², and also reviewed the sketch plan of the property by the plaintiff's agent in preparation for the necessary valuation of the property according to market price.
